metadata.description: 6-module certification on value-based selling methodology aligned to Informativ's E3 sales philosophy (Empathize, Educate, Empower) and Simon-Kucher monetization recommendations. Teaches reps to sell outcomes, not features.

modules.title: The Value Mindset — Selling Outcomes, Not Features
modules.description: Foundation of value selling: the E3 philosophy (Empathize, Educate, Empower), three primary value drivers (Make More, Save More, Stay Safe), and shifting from feature-based to outcome-based selling.
key_concepts: E3 Sales Philosophy: Empathize with challenges, Educate on market/solutions, Empower confident decisions
Three Value Drivers: Make More, Save More, Stay Safe
Value selling = selling outcomes aligned to dealer concerns
Four dealer concerns: Compliance Risk, Fraud Exposure, Sales Inefficiency, Credit Costs
Feature-based selling loses to value-based selling in competitive deals

questions.question: What does E3 stand for in Informativ's sales philosophy?

options.B: Empathize, Educate, Empower

questions.question: What are Informativ's three primary value drivers?

options.B: Make More, Save More, Stay Safe
options.D: Sell More, Spend Less, Win Deals

questions.question: Value selling means:

options.B: Selling outcomes aligned to the dealer's concerns, not listing features

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four key dealer concerns Informativ addresses?

options.B: Fraud Exposure

questions.question: A rep walks into a dealer and immediately starts listing SmartPencil features. What is wrong with this approach?

options.B: The rep should be selling outcomes connected to the dealer's specific pain, not listing features
options.C: SmartPencil should never be mentioned first
options.D: The rep should focus on pricing instead

questions.question: What does 'Empower' mean in the E3 framework?

options.B: Help them make a confident, defensible decision

questions.question: Which value driver addresses protecting the dealership from regulatory, legal, and reputational risk?

options.B: Save More

questions.question: True or False: Value selling requires understanding the dealer's pain before recommending a solution.

options.B: False

modules.title: Persona-Based Discovery & Tier Mapping
modules.description: Using the Persona Discovery Guide to match discovered pain to the right package tier. Covers discovery by buyer role (GM, F&I, Desk, Compliance Officer), the Compliance Risk & Opportunity Audit, and tier alignment methodology.
key_concepts: Discovery determines tier alignment — not the rep's preference or pricing pressure
Three diagnostic questions: What risks? What inefficiency? What's the cost of doing nothing?
Persona-based discovery by role: GM, F&I Director, Desk Manager, Compliance Officer
Compliance Risk & Opportunity Audit creates urgency
Document findings in Salesforce
Pain → Tier Alignment → ROI → Quote Confidence

questions.question: What determines which tier is right for a customer?

options.B: Discovery — matching discovered pain to the right package

questions.question: What are the three key diagnostic questions during discovery?

options.B: What risks are they carrying? What inefficiency slows them? What's the cost of doing nothing?
options.C: How many vehicles, how many stores, what's the budget?
options.D: Who's the competitor, what's the price, when can we close?

questions.question: A GM is focused on deal velocity and staff adoption. Which concern should a rep lead with?

options.B: Will this slow down my team or speed us up?

questions.question: What tool creates urgency by surfacing compliance gaps?

options.B: Battle Card

questions.question: An F&I Director's primary concern is:

options.B: Compliance liability, product penetration, and lender relationships

questions.question: True or False: Reps should document discovery findings in Salesforce before recommending a tier.

options.B: False

questions.question: The correct value selling sequence is:

options.B: Discovered pain → Tier alignment → ROI → Quote confidence
options.D: Cold call → Send pricing → Follow up

questions.question: A dealer says 'Just give me your best price.' How should a value-selling rep respond?

options.B: Redirect to discovery — understand their pain first to recommend the right solution and anchor on value

modules.title: Feature-Advantage-Benefit Framework
modules.description: Translating Informativ's features into advantages and quantified business benefits. Covers the FAB framework for all major platform capabilities and how to use it in sales conversations.
key_concepts: Feature = what it does. Advantage = why it matters. Benefit = quantified business impact.
TotalSolution Platform: unified platform → fewer vendors/logins → saves time and money
Compliance Automation: automated workflows → no manual tracking → reduces legal risk
Mobile DL Scanning: scan from any device → faster intake → reduces fraud risk
Website Credit App: plug-and-play soft pull → instant prequalification → increases lead quality
Real-Time Deal Alerts: centralized dashboard → live oversight → prevents deal delays
Always lead with the benefit, then explain the feature that delivers it

questions.question: In the Feature-Advantage-Benefit framework, what is the correct order?

options.B: Feature → Advantage → Benefit

questions.question: What is the BENEFIT of Informativ's unified TotalSolution Platform?

options.A: It has a nice user interface
options.B: It saves time and money while improving operational efficiency across F&I, Sales, and Compliance teams

questions.question: The advantage of automated compliance workflows (Compliance (Dealer Safeguard)) is:

options.A: It looks good on a demo
options.B: Ensures regulatory alignment without manual tracking
options.D: It eliminates the need for a compliance officer

questions.question: When presenting to a dealer, a value seller should lead with:

options.B: The benefit — quantified business impact — then explain which feature delivers it

questions.question: The benefit of mobile driver's license scanning is:

options.B: Reduces fraud risk and enhances customer satisfaction during the initial interaction

questions.question: What is the benefit of website credit application integration?

options.B: Increases lead quality and showroom traffic with pre-qualified buyers

questions.question: True or False: Features alone close deals in competitive selling environments.

options.B: False

questions.question: A dealer asks 'What does the Real-Time Deal Alerts dashboard do?' A value seller responds with:

options.A: It shows a dashboard with alerts
options.B: It prevents deal delays and enhances accountability — managers get live oversight of all open deals so nothing falls through the cracks

modules.title: Proof Points & ROI Framework
modules.description: Using the ROI Calculator, battle card statistics, and quantified business impact in live sales conversations and proposals. Covers fraud savings, compliance risk reduction, operational efficiency, and revenue impact metrics.
key_concepts: Anchor every recommendation to quantified business impact
Four ROI categories: Fraud savings, Compliance risk reduction, Operational efficiency, Revenue impact
Fraud savings: documented per-incident cost avoidance
Compliance risk: FTC enforcement exposure and lender liability
Operational efficiency: time savings per deal, reduced manual steps
Revenue impact: improved look-to-book, higher PVR, better lead conversion
Use ROI Calculator in proposals to make value tangible

questions.question: What are the four categories of ROI that reps should quantify?

options.B: Fraud savings, Compliance risk reduction, Operational efficiency, Revenue impact

questions.question: When should the ROI Calculator be used?

options.A: Only after the contract is signed
options.B: In live sales conversations and proposals to make value tangible
options.C: Only if the customer asks about pricing

questions.question: Compliance risk reduction ROI focuses on:

options.B: FTC enforcement exposure and lender liability avoidance

questions.question: What does 'improved look-to-book' measure?

options.B: The ratio of customers who visit vs. those who buy — a revenue impact metric

questions.question: A dealer asks 'Why should I pay more for Elevate when Protect seems fine?' The best response uses:

options.A: A discount to close the deal
options.B: Quantified ROI — time savings per deal, reduced compliance violations, faster funding

questions.question: True or False: Every recommendation to a dealer should be anchored to quantified business impact.

options.B: False

questions.question: Operational efficiency ROI is measured by:

options.B: Time savings per deal and reduced manual steps

questions.question: What makes a battle card statistic effective in a sales conversation?

options.B: It connects a data point to a specific dealer pain and quantifies the cost of inaction

modules.title: Objection Handling by Package & Sales Stage
modules.description: Handling objections organized by tier (Protect, Enrich, Elevate, Control), sales stage (Discovery, Demo, Proposal, Negotiation, Close), and objection type (Price, Competitor, Status Quo, Timing). Pre-emptive objection blocking through expertise and value anchoring.
key_concepts: Objections are organized by: Tier, Sales stage, and Objection type
Four objection types: Price, Competitor, Status Quo, Timing
Pre-emptive blocking: establish expertise and anchor value BEFORE resistance surfaces
Price objections: redirect to value and cost of inaction
Competitor objections: differentiate on platform integration and compliance depth
Status quo objections: quantify the cost of doing nothing
Timing objections: create urgency through regulatory risk and competitive pressure

questions.question: What are the four types of objections reps should prepare for?

options.B: Price, Competitor, Status Quo, Timing

questions.question: The best way to handle price objections is to:

options.B: Redirect to value — quantify what the dealer loses by not solving the problem

questions.question: Pre-emptive objection blocking means:

options.B: Establishing expertise and anchoring value before resistance surfaces
options.D: Letting the customer bring up all concerns first

questions.question: A dealer says 'We're fine with our current setup.' This is a:

options.B: Competitor objection

questions.question: How should a rep handle a status quo objection?

options.B: Quantify the cost of doing nothing — what is their current fraud exposure, compliance risk, or operational waste?

questions.question: A dealer says 'We're looking at 700Credit too.' This is a:

options.B: Competitor objection

questions.question: The strongest approach to competitor objections is:

options.B: Differentiate on platform integration, compliance depth, and total solution value

questions.question: True or False: Objection handling is most effective when value has been established early in the sales process.

options.B: False

questions.question: A dealer says 'Can we revisit this next quarter?' How should a rep create urgency?

options.A: Say 'Sure, call me when you're ready'
options.B: Quantify regulatory risk exposure and competitive pressure — what does three more months of non-compliance cost?

modules.title: Quote Confidence & Deal Execution
modules.description: The Quote Confidence Checklist, ensuring discovery confirms the right tier, documenting value in Salesforce, accurate CPQ, CS handoff preparation, and executing the complete value selling process from prospect to close.
key_concepts: Quote Confidence Checklist: 5 verification areas before finalizing
Discovery confirms the right tier (Protect, Enrich, Elevate, or Control)
Value documented in Salesforce with discovered pain and ROI
Objections handled with logic and evidence
CPQ accuracy verified
CS handoff prepped with implementation details
Six-stage sales process: Prospect → Discovery → Demo → Proposal → Negotiation → Close

questions.question: Before finalizing a deal, what must a rep verify using the Quote Confidence Checklist?

options.A: Only that the price is competitive
options.B: Discovery confirms the right tier, value is documented, objections handled, CPQ accurate, CS handoff prepped
options.C: That the customer signed the contract
options.D: Only that the discount was approved

questions.question: Where should discovered pain and ROI be documented?

options.B: In Salesforce
options.C: In an email to the customer

questions.question: What does CPQ stand for in the context of deal execution?

options.B: Configure, Price, Quote

questions.question: Why is the CS handoff important to prep before closing?

options.B: It ensures a smooth implementation and protects the customer relationship — the sale doesn't end at signature

questions.question: If discovery reveals the customer is a good fit for Elevate but the rep quotes Protect to make the deal easier, what's the risk?

options.A: No risk — Protect is always safe
options.B: Margin erosion, customer underserved, likely churn when pain isn't solved, and missed revenue
options.C: The customer will be impressed by the savings

questions.question: True or False: A rep should always lead with the lowest tier to avoid sticker shock.

options.B: False — lead with the tier that matches discovered pain and let value justify the price

questions.question: What is the purpose of documenting value in Salesforce before quoting?

options.B: To create a defensible record connecting discovered pain to the recommended tier and ROI
options.C: To avoid using the Quote Confidence Checklist

questions.question: The six-stage value selling process in order is:

options.A: Demo → Discovery → Quote → Close → Implement → Review
options.B: Prospect → Discovery → Demo → Proposal → Negotiation → Close
options.C: Cold Call → Email → Meeting → Quote → Discount → Close
options.D: Research → Call → Present → Negotiate → Sign → Forget
